page: Microsoft Azure E20s_v7 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the Esv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 20 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 10 physical Intel Xeon 6973P-C cores with hyper-threading enabled. Operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz, the processor is equipped with a large 480 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. The instance is configured with 160.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

In benchmark evaluations, the E20s_v7 demonstrates strong single-threaded performance, achieving top-tier results in gzip compression at 35.43 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 14.68 GB/sec. It also exhibits top-tier performance in LLM text generation for small 135M models, reaching 364.6 tokens/sec. Other benchmarks, such as stress-ng single-core (2232.95) and multi-core (20536.75) CPU performance, fall within the average tier. Memory bandwidth is measured at 1518.31 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 139.09 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Database operations and Redis SET performance (34.54 million operations/sec) also align with mid-range expectations.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E20s_v7 offers a high density of memory resources relative to its compute allocation, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads without paying for excess CPU cores. The inclusion of Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) provides hardware acceleration for vector and matrix operations, aiding in lightweight AI inference. This server is well-suited for memory-intensive applications, including relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, data analytics, and small-scale language model inference.
description: Microsoft Azure E20s_v7 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the Esv7 family, powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor. It features 20 dedicated vCPUs (10 physical cores) and 160.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 8.0 GB per vCPU memory ratio. While it lacks local storage and bundled GPUs, it supports Premium Storage. In benchmarks, the instance delivers top-tier single-threaded performance, notably in gzip compression (35.43 GB/sec) and small-model LLM text generation (364.6 tokens/sec). Standard compute and memory bandwidth benchmarks, including a Redis throughput of 34.54 million operations/sec, place it in the average tier. Supported by Intel AMX and AVX-512 extensions, the E20s_v7 is highly efficient for memory-intensive workloads, such as database hosting, in-memory caching, and data processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E20s_v7 is a memory-optimized VM featuring 20 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 160 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz clock speed, delivering top-tier single-threaded compression and small-model LLM text generation.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E20s_v7 is a memory-optimized VM with 20 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 160 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz clock speed, ideal for database workloads.
tagline: An Intel-powered, memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high-frequency cores and advanced matrix extensions for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 20 dedicated vCPUs powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor
160.0 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per vCPU memory ratio
Top-tier single-threaded performance in gzip compression and small-model LLM text generation
Intel AMX and AVX-512 hardware acceleration for vector and matrix computations
Premium Storage capable with zero bundled local storage or public IPv4 addresses
Optimized for relational databases, in-memory caching, and data analytics
